source.title: Sensorimotor beta power reflects the precision-weighting afforded to sensory prediction errors

confidence_rationale: This is the most direct empirical support for the core MECH-057a mechanism. Palmer et al. measured EEG beta power during a visuomotor adaptation task with manipulated sensory uncertainty, and found that sensorimotor beta power correlates with inverse uncertainty (precision) afforded to sensory prediction errors -- both prior to and following a movement. The interpretation is explicit: beta oscillations encode relative uncertainty (precision) within the sensorimotor system, not just error magnitude. This is precisely the MECH-057a mechanism -- beta suppresses sensory precision reweighting, not motor output. The paper provides a computational (Bayesian/HGF) link between beta power and the precision parameter that MECH-057a predicts should be suppressed during committed sequences. The MECH-090 / MECH-057a distinction (beta blocks precision propagation vs. motor output) is supported by this result.
failure_signatures: Beta correlated with error magnitude rather than precision -- would support MECH-090 but weaken MECH-057a's specific precision-suppression framing
The precision effect was found to be post-movement only -- would suggest beta encodes error evaluation not commitment-phase suppression
